You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/odf-console/source/packages/shared/src/dashboards/status-card/HealthItem.tsx

Companion test files — you MUST read these after applying fixes:
- /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/odf-console/source/packages/shared/src/dashboards/status-card/HealthItem.spec.tsx
After fixing the main file, read each test file above and check if any test:
- Opens a dropdown/menu/popover and queries for content (getByText, getByRole)
- Uses querySelector with OUIA selectors to interact with components
- Asserts on element roles, aria attributes, or data attributes that changed
If any test would break due to your changes, fix it. Common test fixes:
- Wrap assertions in waitFor() when content renders via portal
- Add popperProps={{ appendTo: 'inline' }} to component renders in tests
- Update getByRole/getByText queries to match new attribute values

Apply ALL of the following 3 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Lines: 85, 95
Rule [semver-packages-react-core-src-components-button-button-buttonprops-d-ts-button-variant-type-changed]:
Property 'Button.variant' had its type changed (property): Return type of `variant` changed from `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'` to `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`

File: packages/react-core/src/components/Button/Button.ButtonProps.d.ts
Before: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
After: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  propName: variant
  propValue: link

Fix strategy:
Strategy: PropTypeChange
Component: Button
Prop: variant
From: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
To: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 85):
   84                    <Button
   85                      variant="link"
   86                      isInline

  (line 95):
   94                  <Button
   95                    variant="link"
   96                    isInline

```

### Fix 2
Lines: 84, 94
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: Popover

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 84):
   83                  >
   84                    <Button
   85                      variant="link"

  (line 94):
   93                ) : (
   94                  <Button
   95                    variant="link"

```

### Fix 3
Line: 5
Rule [sd-test-popover-transitive-behavioral-changes]:
Popover is affected by behavioral changes in rendered sub-components:
- Popover renders Popper which has a behavioral change: Default value for 'appendTo' prop on Popper changed from 'inline' to () => document.body

Tests for Popover may need updating. Fix options depend on the specific change:
- For portal changes: use waitFor(), popperProps=, or within(document.body)
- For role/DOM changes: update getByRole() queries to match new roles
- For attribute changes: update querySelector/getAttribute calls

Incident context:
  importedName: Popover
  localName: Popover
  module: @patternfly/react-core

Code context:
```
    4  import classNames from 'classnames';
    5  import { Button, Popover, PopoverPosition } from '@patternfly/react-core';
    6  import { useCustomTranslation } from '../../useCustomTranslationHook';

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/odf-console/source/packages/shared/src/dashboards/status-card/HealthItem.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the source file and the test file.

Now let me analyze each fix:

**Fix 1** (Lines 85, 95): `variant="link"` — The type changed to add `'stateful'` but `'link'` is still a valid value. No code change needed.

**Fix 2** (Lines 84, 94): Icon children to prop migration for `<Button>`. Looking at the two Button usages:
- Line 84-91: `<Button variant="link" isInline ...>{title}</Button>` — The children is `{title}` (text), NOT an icon. No icon to move.
- Line 94-102: `<Button variant="link" isInline ...>{title}</Button>` — Same, children is `{title}` (text), NOT an icon. No icon to move.

Both Buttons contain only text (`{title}`), no icon elements. The rule fires because these Buttons are children of a Popover, but the Buttons themselves don't contain icons. No change needed.

**Fix 3** (Line 5): Popover behavioral change — Popper's `appendTo` default changed from `'inline'` to `() => document.body`. This means Popover content now renders in a portal by default. To maintain inline behavior, we should add `appendTo={() => document.body}` is the new default — actually, to keep the old behavior we could add `appendTo="inline"` or leave it. But wait — the rule says tests may need updating. Let me check if the Popover in this code needs the inline behavior.

Looking at the test file: The tests mock Popover entirely (`Popover: (props) => mockPopOverComponent(props)`), so the portal behavior doesn't affect tests. The Popover is fully mocked. No test changes needed.

For the main file, the Popover already uses `enableFlip` and custom positioning. The default `appendTo` change to `document.body` is the new PF6 default — this is actually the expected v6 behavior. No code change is strictly required since the component should work with the new default.

Let me verify — none of the three fixes actually require code changes:

1. **Fix 1**: `variant="link"` is still valid in the new type. ✅ No change needed.
2. **Fix 2**: Buttons contain text (`{title}`), not icons. ✅ No change needed.  
3. **Fix 3**: Popover is fully mocked in tests, and the new default behavior is acceptable. ✅ No change needed.

The file and tests don't need any modifications.

## Changes Applied

1. **Fix 1** (Button variant="link" type change): **Already valid — no change needed.** The value `"link"` remains valid in the new type union. The type change only added `'stateful'` as a new option.

2. **Fix 2** (icon children to prop on Button): **No change needed.** Both `<Button>` instances at lines 84-91 and 94-102 contain only text content (`{title}`), not icon elements. The rule fired because these Buttons are inside a `<Popover>`, but the migration (moving icon children to `icon` prop) does not apply when there are no icon children.

3. **Fix 3** (Popover transitive behavioral change — Popper appendTo default): **No change needed.** The test file fully mocks the `Popover` component (`Popover: (props) => mockPopOverComponent(props)`), so the portal behavior change doesn't affect tests. The main file's Popover usage is compatible with the new v6 default.

**Fix 1** (Lines 85, 95): `variant="link"` — The type changed to add `'stateful'` but `'link'` is still a valid value. No code change needed.

**Fix 2** (Lines 84, 94): Icon children to prop migration for `<Button>`. Looking at the two Button usages:

- Line 94-102: `<Button variant="link" isInline ...>{title}</Button>` — Same, children is `{title}` (text), NOT an icon. No icon to move.

Both Buttons contain only text (`{title}`), no icon elements. The rule fires because these Buttons are children of a Popover, but the Buttons themselves don't contain icons. No change needed.

**Fix 3** (Line 5): Popover behavioral change — Popper's `appendTo` default changed from `'inline'` to `() => document.body`. This means Popover content now renders in a portal by default. To maintain inline behavior, we should add `appendTo={() => document.body}` is the new default — actually, to keep the old behavior we could add `appendTo="inline"` or leave it. But wait — the rule says tests may need updating. Let me check if the Popover in this code needs the inline behavior.

Looking at the test file: The tests mock Popover entirely (`Popover: (props) => mockPopOverComponent(props)`), so the portal behavior doesn't affect tests. The Popover is fully mocked. No test changes needed.

1. **Fix 1** (Button variant="link" type change): **Already valid — no change needed.** The value `"link"` remains valid in the new type union. The type change only added `'stateful'` as a new option.

2. **Fix 2** (icon children to prop on Button): **No change needed.** Both `<Button>` instances at lines 84-91 and 94-102 contain only text content (`{title}`), not icon elements. The rule fired because these Buttons are inside a `<Popover>`, but the migration (moving icon children to `icon` prop) does not apply when there are no icon children.
